name: Venezuela
avoid: May to November is wetter and less predictable for Canaima river runs, Los Roques boat days, and mountain views in Mérida.
Venezuela's safety and political situation remains fragile: avoid protests, do not use unregulated airport taxis, and keep intercity moves in daylight with hotel-arranged transfers where possible.
Domestic flight schedules, fuel availability, and card acceptance can change quickly, so carry backup cash in small notes and keep same-day slack before every island or park flight.
stopoverNote: Caracas is the practical hub for Conviasa hops to Canaima, Los Roques, and Porlamar, so most multi-stop Venezuela itineraries work best as out-and-back spokes from the capital.

cities.name: Caracas
cities.notes: Main air hub for Venezuela, with the Warairarepano cable car, Bolívar-era landmarks, and the easiest base for onward domestic flights.

cities.name: Canaima
cities.notes: Remote Pemón gateway to Laguna de Canaima and Salto Ángel, where most stays bundle flights, meals, guides, and boat logistics.

cities.name: Mérida
cities.notes: Andean university city known for the Mukumbarí cable car, cool weather, markets, and easy mountain viewpoints.

cities.name: Los Roques
cities.notes: Gran Roque base for reef flats, white-sand cays, snorkeling, kitesurfing, and posada-based island logistics.

cities.name: Isla de Margarita
cities.notes: Beach-and-resort island around Porlamar and Pampatar, mixing Caribbean strands, castles, seafood, and easy domestic-flight access.

itinerary.sim: Digitel is usually the easiest tourist SIM/eSIM option; expect roughly ₹830–₹1,660 for a small 4–10GB pack, but coverage weakens quickly in Canaima, offshore cays, and mountain roads, so download offline maps before leaving Caracas.

Caracas.name: Caracas
Caracas.note: Stay in Altamira, Los Palos Grandes, or La Castellana, use hotel-booked cars or app rides after dark, and avoid flashing valuables around bus terminals or airport ATMs.

activities.name: Teleférico Warairarepano round trip (international tourist fare)
Plaza Bolívar, Catedral de Caracas, and Casa Natal del Libertador
Paseo at Hotel Humboldt viewpoints or Galipán ridge stops
Museo de Bellas Artes or nearby Parque Los Caobos
Parque Generalísimo Francisco de Miranda morning walk
Centro de Arte Los Galpones galleries and coffee stops
Museo de Arte Contemporáneo de Caracas
Evening in Las Mercedes with hotel-arranged return transfer

Canaima.name: Canaima
Canaima.note: Book full-board lodges or a verified operator before flying in, carry the national park fee in cash, and keep an extra buffer day because wind and river levels can shift Salto Ángel departures.

Laguna de Canaima curiara ride and El Sapo or El Hacha waterfall walk
Laguna beach sunset under the tepuis
Pemón craft stalls near the lagoon
Shared Salto Ángel river expedition from Canaima
Curiara ride up the Carrao and Churún rivers
Mirador Laime trek for the classic Angel Falls view
Hammock-camp night or simple jungle lodge depending on river level
Optional helicopter or scenic overflight over the tepuis
Easy canoeing and photography around Laguna de Canaima
Free time for weather delays before the return flight
Lodge-guided stargazing if skies stay clear

Mérida.name: Mérida
Mérida.note: Bring layers for cool evenings, pre-check Teleférico Mukumbarí operating days before arriving, and use registered taxis or por puestos for side trips to Jají or mountain viewpoints.

activities.name: Teleférico Mukumbarí foreign visitor ticket
Plaza Las Heroínas and Mérida cathedral quarter
Mercado Principal de Mérida snacks and produce walk
Evening stop at Heladería Coromoto
Por puesto or registered taxi round trip to Jají
Jají colonial square and mountain viewpoints
Museo Arquidiocesano or city-center church circuit
Slow afternoon back in Mérida for cafés and artisan shops
Shared car or por puesto toward Mucuchíes and páramo viewpoints
Capilla de Piedra de Juan Félix Sánchez
Local trucha lunch in the highlands
Return before dusk because mountain weather changes fast

Los Roques.name: Los Roques
Los Roques.note: Most posadas on Gran Roque include breakfast and dinner, boats leave early around wind and tide, and you should reconfirm baggage limits because light-aircraft allowances are strict.

activities.name: Conviasa Caracas–Los Roques round-trip flight
Boat hop to Madrisquí or Francisquí
Snorkel straight off the beach in calm conditions
Gran Roque lighthouse sunset walk

days.theme: Cayo de Agua or Crasquí Day

activities.name: Full-day lancha to Cayo de Agua or Crasquí with beach time
Snorkel gear rental
Fresh seafood lunch arranged by your posada or boat crew
Evening lobster or catch-of-the-day dinner back on Gran Roque

meals: Play Los Roques - Bar & Restaurante & Kite Center

days.theme: Wind Backup & Gran Roque Slow Day

activities.name: Short boat run to Noronquí or quiet beach if weather allows
Kitesurf or paddle add-on in season
Stroll village lanes and artisan shops around Plaza Bolívar

Isla de Margarita.name: Isla de Margarita
Isla de Margarita.note: Base yourself around Pampatar, Costa Azul, or a full-service beach resort, pre-arrange airport transfers, and keep beach days flexible if domestic flights shift on the Caracas leg.

activities.name: Castillo San Carlos de Borromeo and Pampatar seafront
Playa El Agua or Playa Parguito beach day
Pampatar sunset boardwalk and old quarter

Basílica Menor de Nuestra Señora del Valle
La Asunción historic center and Castillo Santa Rosa
Evening seafood dinner back in Pampatar
Conviasa Caracas–Porlamar round-trip flight
Costa Azul or Playa Moreno easy swim day
Sambil Margarita shopping or café stop
Use as a spare day for weather or flight padding
Registered taxi day trip toward Punta Arenas and the Macanao side
Quiet beach time away from the Porlamar strip
Seafood lunch en route

connections.method: Direct Conviasa flight from Caracas to Canaima (about 1h 5m), usually with strict baggage limits and only a few operating days each week
Return flight from Canaima to Caracas, then continue by SITSSA overnight bus to Mérida or by a domestic flight toward El Vigía plus road transfer
SITSSA bus or por puesto back toward Caracas, then Conviasa or charter flight onward to Los Roques; keep a night in Caracas if you do not want a risky same-day connection
Fly back to Caracas from Gran Roque, then connect to Porlamar on Conviasa; this is usually an all-day transfer and works best with a schedule buffer

extras: Best timing is December to April for the driest mix across Canaima, Los Roques, and the Mérida highlands; shoulder-season showers are workable but make boat days and mountain views less reliable.

Travel advisory: Venezuela remains a high-risk destination because crime, political unrest, and limited healthcare can affect plans quickly. Recheck current government advisories before departure, avoid demonstrations, and use only registered transfers or hotel-arranged taxis, especially around Maiquetía airport.
Domestic tourism is heavily dollarized, but card terminals and mobile data fail often; carry cash in small USD notes and expect some fees to be settled at the day's bolívar exchange rate.
Conviasa flights are practical for Canaima, Los Roques, and Porlamar, yet schedules can move with demand, weather, or operational changes, so avoid tight same-day international connections.

Long-distance buses remain the cheapest way to reach Mérida, while por puesto shared taxis are useful for short regional hops like Jají or Mucuchíes when you want more flexibility than scheduled buses.
Pack strong sun protection for the islands, a light rain shell for Canaima spray and sudden storms, and mosquito repellent for lagoon and jungle nights.
